title: Role of a Parliamentarian
intro: “Parliament is not a rubber stamp for the executive; it is a mirror of the people's will, and the Member of Parliament is its most vital reflecting surface.”

items.title: Legislative
bullets: Introducing, debating, voting on Bills — Government and ==Private Member's Bills==
Scrutinising ==delegated legislation==
Select/Joint Committees: clause-by-clause examination

items.title: Representative
bullets: Voices constituency, regional and social-group concerns in the national forum
Link between ==citizens and the state== — grievances, scheme access

items.title: Deliberative
bullets: Debates policy, budgets, public importance — ==Zero Hour==, Short Duration Discussions, Calling Attention Motions
==‘Safety valve’== for public opinion and dissent

items.title: Oversight / Accountability
bullets: ==Question Hour==, No-Confidence & Adjournment Motions
Committee scrutiny: ==DRSCs, PAC, Estimates, PUC==; CAG reports via PAC

items.title: Financial Control
bullets: Approves Budget, Demands for Grants, Money Bills — ==‘power of the purse’==
Ancestry: Commons' ==‘no taxation without representation’==

items.title: Constituency Development
bullets: ==MPLADS — ₹5 crore p.a. per MP== for local infrastructure
Grievance redressal via ministries; privilege to raise local issues

items.title: Quasi-Judicial / Disciplinary
bullets: Impeachment of judges/President; privilege matters
Disqualification under the ==Tenth Schedule==

items.label: MPs / MLAs with serious criminal charges declared
Bills referred to DRSCs in 17th LS (was 60–70% in 14th–15th)

blocks.text: Most common professions among MPs: ==agriculture and social work==.

items.title: A · Declining Productivity & Scrutiny
bullets: Sitting days: ==~127/yr in the 1950s → under 60–70== now
Committee referral collapsed: ==~16% in 17th LS vs 60%+ earlier==
Rushed passage: the three ==farm laws (2020, repealed)== — minimal debate, no committee reference

items.title: B · Disruptions & Lost Time
bullets: Disruption as routine opposition tool; sessions at ==<50% of scheduled time==
2016: RS couldn't take up Finance & Appropriation Bills amid protests
==Question Hour washouts== erode oversight itself

items.title: C · Anti-Defection & the Whip
bullets: ==Tenth Schedule (52nd CA, 1985)==: MPs vote per party diktat — legislators become party instruments
Undermines executive accountability, deliberation, and MP–voter accountability (‘blame the whip’)
Engineering persists: ==Karnataka 2019, Maharashtra 2022, TN AIADMK 2026, Telangana BRS→INC 2024–25==

items.title: D · Weak Committee System
bullets: DRSC attendance ~49–50%; ==one-year tenure== blocks expertise
PAC recommendations non-binding (though ~80% accepted)
Thin research staff vs the US Congress

items.title: E · Criminalisation of Politics
bullets: ==ADR (2019 LS): ~43% of MPs with criminal cases, ~29% serious==
Erodes deliberative quality and public trust

items.title: F · Office of Profit
bullets: Ambiguity in ==Arts 102(1)(a), 191(1)(a)==
==20 Delhi MLAs disqualified (2018)== — Parliamentary Secretary posts

items.title: G · Constituency Overload
bullets: Electoral incentives reward visible ==MPLADS delivery== over legislative scrutiny
Structural tension: representative vs deliberative functions

items.title: H · Privileges: Ambiguity & Misuse
bullets: Privileges uncodified → uncertainty
[[Sita Soren v. UoI (2024)]] overturned [[P.V. Narasimha Rao (1998)]]: ==no immunity from bribery prosecution== for a vote/speech

head: Country
Practice
Relevance for India
rows: MPs may break a ==three-line whip on conscience issues==; cross-party select committees, often opposition-chaired
Calibrated whip: confidence votes vs ordinary legislation
==Permanent committees with large expert staff==; deep specialisation from long tenures
Longer DRSC tenures + dedicated research staff
==‘Conscience votes’== officially recognised on moral/ethical issues
Codified select-committee rules; ==mandatory public bill referrals==

items.title: Structural
bullets: ==Mandatory committee referral== of non-urgent Bills (UK)
Extend DRSC tenure beyond one year (US)
Codify privileges — ==Privileges Committee, LS (2008)==

items.title: Behavioural / Political
bullets: Confine whips to confidence/money matters — ==170th Law Commission (1999); NCRWC (2002)==
Intra-party democracy — ==II ARC (2007)==
Time-bound anti-defection adjudication — [[Keisham Meghachandra Singh v. Speaker (2020)]]

items.title: Institutional Capacity
bullets: Expert committee staff — ==US Congressional Research Service== model
Public ==MP performance dashboards== (spirit of PRS MP Track)
Research support per MP — UK ==House of Commons Library==

items.title: Electoral / Accountability
bullets: Reward deliberative performance, not just MPLADS visibility
==Fast-track criminal cases== against sitting legislators (SC directions)

blocks.text: The parliamentarian is simultaneously a legislator, a representative, a watchdog, and a constituency servant — a role demanding both national vision and local responsiveness.
